---
title: "Debugging"
description: "Read errors calmly, inspect state, and make small experiments instead of guessing."
execute:
  echo: true
categories: [debugging, beginner]
order: 230
---

## Why this chapter matters

Read errors calmly, inspect state, and make small experiments instead of guessing.

Lessons are now grouped by coherent learner topics. A page may contain multiple
short quizzes when the page has multiple sections, so students can check one
idea before moving to the next.

::: {.callout-note title="Guiding questions"}
- Which lessons belong in this chapter?
- What should I review before moving on?
- How do the lesson sections build one practical skill?
:::

## Lesson path

- [Error Messages](error-messages.qmd) — Read tracebacks and recognize common beginner error patterns.
- [Debugging Tools](debugging-tools.qmd) — Use print debugging, VS Code breakpoints, and pdb to inspect running programs.
- [Minimal Reproducible Examples](minimal-reproducible-examples.qmd) — Shrink a bug into the smallest code that still shows the problem.

## Chapter checkpoint

::: {.quiz-box}
Use this short checkpoint before starting the chapter.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "debugging-overview",
  "title": "Debugging overview checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose an answer, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "chapter-purpose",
      "prompt": "What is the purpose of the Debugging chapter?",
      "options": [
        "Read errors calmly, inspect state, and make small experiments instead of guessing.",
        "To memorize disconnected commands",
        "To skip practice",
        "To avoid debugging"
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Read errors calmly, inspect state, and make small experiments instead of guessing."
    },
    {
      "id": "study-flow",
      "prompt": "Why can a lesson contain multiple quizzes?",
      "options": [
        "Each quiz checks one section before the next section builds on it.",
        "Quizzes replace examples.",
        "Multiple quizzes mean the topic is unrelated.",
        "Quizzes should only appear at the end of the course."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Section-level quizzes help learners review while the idea is still fresh."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include ../_includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}
